preamble: Consonantalization and fricativization of the high back vowel [u] or its semivowel reflex [w] into the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] is a well-documented process in historical linguistics, notably in the development of Latin and Germanic sounds.

examples.note: The Latin letter 'V' represented both the vowel and the semivowel, which eventually fortified into fricative consonants in various daughter languages.
Part of the broader systemic shifts in the early history of Germanic languages where glides became fricatives (e.g., initial labials).
